items.title: ABS estimates 80pc of tobacco consumed in Australia last year illegal amid 'rapid growth' in black market
items.summary: The experimental estimates are based on "nicotine metabolite concentrations" detected in wastewater samples, combined with data showing a collapse in spending on legal cigarettes and vapes.

items.title: People with intellectual disability continue to receive substandard healthcare as program to train doctors risks closure
items.summary: Sam Stubbs’s parents were asked about his quality of life while treating him for a lung infection. They realised it was because he was born with Down syndrome.

items.title: Quilpie builders experiment with 'flat-pack' housing to improve supply in the outback
items.summary: A Queensland outback town pins its hopes of solving a local housing crisis on "flat-pack" homes as the high cost of building risks paralysing the community.

items.title: Tasmanians letting fitness regimes slip as cost-of-living pressures rise

items.summary: Before the federal budget, the housing market was already starting to choke on its own excesses. Now some economists are tipping an extended period where home prices grow no faster than general inflation.

items.title: Three Australian men found guilty after taking 'black flight' into Indonesia's South Papua province

items.title: Almost eight months on from the ceasefire, Gaza's residents are still struggling to rebuild a city in ruins
items.summary: It's been seven months since the ceasefire in Gaza, but reconstruction has barely begun, water remains contaminated and minimal aid is getting into the strip. This is what life is like for its residents.
